behaviors.description: Trim conversation history to the most recent 10 messages before sending to the LLM when history is too long.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (history manager -> LLM request builder), not just one pure function in isolation. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; an in-process LLM stub can assert payload. MOCK-HIDE: simple mocks do not inherently hide this; they can still verify exact message list. LLM-DEP: does not depend on model output quality, only request construction. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic sequence required. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about "history passed to the LLM is trimmed" (input shaping), not LLM judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chat handler + history store + LLM client stub. Seed >10 prior messages, send a new user message, and assert the outbound LLM payload includes only the latest 10 history messages (correct order, no older entries).

behaviors.description: Generated response is actually relevant to the customer question (not generic/off-topic).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: relevance failures are not primarily wiring bugs; they are model-behavior failures. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement drives System. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed LLM response would always pass expected relevance checks and hide real off-topic behavior. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM output quality and run-to-run variance. MULTI-STEP: single core generation quality check, so Agentic (not Workflow). Rejected Workflow: behavior text only requires one generation outcome, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real prompt calls on a curated set of customer questions with conversation context; score whether answers directly address the asked issue (rubric/human or LLM-judge with guardrails) and fail if off-topic/generic rate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: Tone re-check identifies an overly formal response for casual-tier users and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is cross-stage behavior (initial generation -> tone evaluation -> regeneration trigger). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network requirement beyond normal API call. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing tone decisions hides whether the real model/checker actually flags over-formality correctly. LLM-DEP: identification of "too formal" is model-quality dependent. MULTI-STEP: requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (first response and follow-up regeneration path), so Workflow is the failure boundary. Rejected Agentic: behavior explicitly includes both correct identification and downstream regeneration trigger, i.e., sequenced agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real LLM on casual-tier prompts known to elicit formal wording; assert the first response is flagged as too formal by the live tone check and that a second generation is invoked and returned.

behaviors.description: Persist full exchange (user message, initial response, any regenerated response) with correct timestamps in the database.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at app -> persistence boundary and record assembly across modules. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem integration is sufficient; no real external network or OS primitive beyond normal local persistence is required. MOCK-HIDE: in-memory fakes may miss schema/transaction mapping issues, so use real local DB adapter. LLM-DEP: persistence correctness does not depend on model quality. MULTI-STEP: multiple records may be written, but this is transactional data flow, not multi-agentic reasoning. Rejected System: behavior text is DB persistence/timestamps, which can be validated with local integration DB without subprocess/real external API dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run chat flow with regeneration path enabled against a real local test DB. Assert stored rows include user input, first draft, regenerated draft (if present), and monotonic/correct timestamp fields tied to each event.

behaviors.description: If tone-check triggers regeneration, only the second response is returned to the customer; rejected draft is never exposed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff across modules (generator, tone-check result handler, response publisher). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is needed; in-process wiring can reproduce leakage bugs. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not inherently hide this because fixed first/second responses can still detect whether wrong one is surfaced. LLM-DEP: behavior text conditions on "when tone check triggers" and checks output selection, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: sequence exists, but correctness target is deterministic replacement logic after trigger. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: concrete disqualifier is the behavior wording focuses on visibility rule ("customer never sees the rejected draft") rather than correctness of model judgments/content quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use integrated pipeline with deterministic stubs for first response, tone-trigger=true, and regenerated response. Assert API/UI output contains only regenerated text, while first draft may exist only in internal logs and is never emitted to customer channel.
